$comment: Boundary-case fixture for the distributed-boundary matrix. Not a live release packet. See docs/compat/boundary_matrix.md.
scenario: Launcher-and-local-sidecars boundary running a single coordinated artifact set. Launcher, indexer helper, diagnostic helper, and search helper all come from one coordinated artifact set with shared build identity, contract version, and schema epoch. The supported window is coordinated_artifact_set_only. Rolling upgrades of individual helpers while the launcher stays on the prior set is not a claim on this boundary.

supported_skew_window.summary: Launcher and helper binaries come from one coordinated artifact set with shared build identity, contract version, and schema epoch.

upgrade_order.notes: Promote the full launcher-plus-helpers artifact graph together; mixed startup outside the coordinated set is refused, not warned.

rollback_order.notes: Rollback restores the prior coordinated set, never a partial mix.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: Refuse mixed launcher/helper startup with a visible reason; offer rollback to the prior coordinated artifact set.

what_happens_when_upgrade_order_violated: A mixed set, for example launcher.2026.04 with indexer.2026.02, is not a runnable state. Startup refuses with a visible fail-closed reason citing the coordinated set; the launcher offers rollback to the prior coordinated set. The worked envelope is fixtures/compat/mixed_version_cases/upgrade_order_violation.json, rendered here by fixtures/compat/boundary_cases/refuse_start.json.
what_happens_when_skew_window_exceeded: Equivalent to an upgrade-order violation on this boundary: any mix outside the coordinated set is out of window and refuses to start. There is no degraded mode; coordinated-artifact-set-only boundaries do not run partially.
notes: Illustrative seed boundary case; this is not a live release packet.
